image_prompt: SVG: reaction energy profile diagram. Y-axis 'Energy' (qualitative, no numeric ticks), X-axis 'Progress of reaction'. Two curves both starting at the 'reactants' level and ending lower at the 'products' level (exothermic): an uncatalysed curve (teal) with a tall activation-energy peak, and a catalysed curve (orange) with a lower peak. Vertical double-headed Ea arrows mark each activation energy from the reactant level to each peak. Labels: reactants, products, uncatalysed, catalysed, Ea.

SVG: heating curve for a pure substance solid at 30 °C. X-axis 'Time / minutes' (qualitative, no numeric ticks), Y-axis 'Temperature / °C' ticks 30, 60, 90, 120. Line rises from A(30 °C), levels at 60 °C across B-C (melting plateau), rises to 90 °C at D, levels across D-E (boiling plateau), then rises to F at 120 °C. Points labelled A, B, C, D, E, F.

render_data.title: Heating curve of a pure substance (solid at 30 °C)

image_prompt: SVG: scatter graph of boiling temperature of water versus altitude. X-axis 'Altitude / m' 0 to 4000, ticks every 1000. Y-axis 'Boiling temperature of water / °C' 95 to 100, ticks every 1. Plotted points approx (1,100.0), (210,99.8), (1400,98.6), (2625,97.2), (3640,96.1) and an outlier at (4000,95.7) lying just below the trend. A straight best-fit line drawn from (0,100.0) down to about (4000,95.8), excluding the outlier.
SVG: iron-powder hand-warmer sachet (soft sealed pouch of grey iron powder) heated over flames, with the equation 4Fe + 3O2 -> 2Fe2O3 and orange 'heat released' arrows radiating upward.

SVG: displayed/skeletal structure of the ethanoate (acetate) ion CH3COO- with the methyl group's three C-H bonds, C-C bond, C=O double bond and C-O(-) single bond, plus a separate Na+ cation. Caption 'Sodium ethanoate'.

SVG: two ester skeletal structures - methyl butanoate (C3H7COOCH3, aroma of apple) shown beside a red apple, and pentyl ethanoate (CH3COOC5H11, aroma of pear) shown beside a green pear. Each ester drawn with its zig-zag carbon chain, ester C-O-C linkage and C=O double bond.

SVG: the acid and alcohol that form pentyl ethanoate - ethanoic acid (CH3COOH) skeletal structure on the left and pentan-1-ol (C5H11OH) skeletal structure on the right, with the note 'acid + alcohol -> pentyl ethanoate + water'.

SVG: particle diagrams of the three states of matter in glass vessels - SOLID (regular close-packed lattice of particles in a beaker), GAS (sparse scattered particles in a round-bottom flask, some escaping through the neck) and LIQUID (close but irregularly packed particles in a beaker). Each labelled with a 'select' dropdown beneath.

render_data.title: Label the three states of matter

topic: Boiling temperature vs altitude data table
image_prompt: SVG: clean ruled data table 'Location | Altitude / m | Boiling temperature of water / C' with rows Bangkok 1 100.0, Belgrade [blank] 99.8, Bogota 2625 97.2, Canberra 605 [blank], Kathmandu 1400 98.6, La Paz 3640 96.1, Nairobi [blank] 98.0, New Delhi 210 99.8, Tehran 1138 [blank]. Some cells deliberately left blank for the student to complete.

SVG: scene of a weather balloon labelled 'MYP LABS' with a sampling capsule/probe rising in the troposphere over a green landscape with snow-capped mountains; a 'gas sample' marker indicates an air sample being collected to measure oxygen percentage. 'Troposphere' label rotated on the left.

render_data.title: Atmosphere sampling probe — oxygen vs altitude
render_data.task: Launch the probe at each altitude to record the percentage of oxygen, then plot the results.
render_data.note: The data point at 500 km is an outlier (18.0 %) and should be excluded from the line of best fit.

image_prompt: SVG: tablet device showing a 'MYP LABS' data-collection app with a table 'Altitude / km | Oxygen / %' filled in: 10/21.0, 50/18.0, 120/16.0, 200/15.5, 300/15.0, 400/15.0, 500/18.0, 600/15.0. Status line 'Collection complete'.

image_prompt: SVG: clean ruled table of salts used as food additives with columns Salt | Taste | Other use. Rows: ammonium chloride / salty and quite bitter / in licorice and baking to give cookies a crisp texture; calcium chloride / salty and bitter / maintains crispness and firmness of fruits and vegetables; magnesium chloride / salty and bitter / in tofu as a thickener; potassium chloride / salty and slightly bitter / a substitute for sodium chloride; sodium chloride / salty / a flavouring and a preservative.
